name: Eggplant Imam Bayildi
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Currants, soaked in cold water for 30 minutes
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the stems off the eggplants but do not peel them.
2. Make several length-wise slits in the eggplants without slicing through.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a small skillet and saute the onions until they brown slightly.
4. Add tomato, salt, pepper, allspice, and parsley to the pan. Cook over medium-low heat until the mixture breaks down into almost a puree.
5. Drain the currants and add them to the tomato-onion mixture. Cook for an additional 10 minutes.
6. Stuff the cooked mixture into the slits in the eggplants using your fingers or a knife.
7. Place the eggplants in a flame-proof dish that will just hold them.
8. Pour oil over the eggplants until it reaches about halfway up the side of the dish.
9. Add garlic and bay leaf to the oil.
10. Cover the dish and cook over very low heat for an hour, turning the eggplants every 15 minutes until they are very soft.
11. Turn off the heat, pour off any excess oil, and strain the mixture before storing it for later use.
12. Cool the eggplants and refrigerate overnight.
13. Serve cold with thin slices.
